parameters.subject: Your Join request was received
parameters.message: =Hi {{ $("Normalize Email").first().json.senderName }},  thank you for your request.  Your email has been processed and a new ticket was created in the Join board. The ticket has been placed in the Triage column and will be reviewed by the team.  Best regards Join Issue Collector

parameters.subject: Request limit reached
parameters.message: =Hi {{ $("Normalize Email").first().json.senderName }},  your daily request limit has been reached.  You can create up to 10 requests per day. Please try again tomorrow.  Best regards Join Issue Collector

parameters.text:
=You are a strict email-to-task extraction agent for a Kanban board called Join.

Analyze the complete email message below and extract structured task data from it.

Return ONLY raw valid JSON.
No markdown.
No explanations.
No code fences.
No text before or after the JSON.

Current date: {{$("Normalize Email").first().json.today}}

Complete email message:
{{$("Normalize Email").first().json.body}}

Return exactly this JSON structure:

{
  "title": "",
  "description": "",
  "category": "",
  "priority": "",
  "dueDate": "",
  "subtasks": []
}

Rules:

1. Title
- Find the actual request title in the complete email.
- Prefer text after labels like "Request title:", "Title:", "Feature:", or "Issue:".
- If no explicit title exists, create a concise title from the main request.
- Maximum 60 characters.

2. Description
- Extract the main request description.
- Prefer text after "Request description:" or "Description:".
- If there is an "Expected benefit:" section, append it to the description.
- Remove greetings, signatures, labels and empty placeholder text.
- Do not include "Hi Join team", "Best regards" or label names.

3. Category
- Return "Technical Task" for bugs, technical fixes, implementation, refactoring, architecture, CSS, JavaScript, Firebase, API, automation, performance, data or workflow issues.
- Return "User Story" for new features, UX/UI improvements, business requests, user-facing improvements or general product requests.
- If unclear, return "User Story".

4. Priority
- Search the complete email for a priority field or priority wording.
- If it contains "urgent", "critical", "asap", "high priority", "important" or "blocking", return "urgent".
- If it contains "low", "minor", "nice to have" or "not urgent", return "low".
- If it contains "medium", "normal" or "standard", return "medium".
- Only if no priority information exists, return "medium".
- Never return "medium" if the email clearly contains "Urgent".

5. Due date
- Search the complete email for a due date, deadline or timing requirement.
- Convert the result to YYYY-MM-DD.
- Interpret relative dates like "tomorrow", "next Friday", "end of next week" or "in 2 days" based on Current date.
- If no due date exists, return tomorrow based on Current date.
- Never return an empty dueDate.

6. Subtasks
- Search the complete email for suggested subtasks, checklist items, bullet points or implementation steps.
- Extract every task-like bullet point.
- Bullet points may start with "-", "•", "*", numbers or separate lines.
- Stop at greetings or signature text.
- Return each subtask exactly as:
  { "text": "subtask text", "done": false }
- If no subtasks exist, return [].

Valid output example:

{
  "title": "Fix login validation bug",
  "description": "The login form accepts invalid email addresses. This improves data quality.",
  "category": "Technical Task",
  "priority": "urgent",
  "dueDate": "2026-05-16",
  "subtasks": [
    { "text": "Validate email format", "done": false },
    { "text": "Show error message", "done": false }
  ]
}
